---
title: ""
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

<div class="hero">
  <video autoplay muted loop playsinline>
    <source src="/assets/merged_hero.mp4" type="video/mp4">
  </video>
  <div class="hero-overlay"></div>
  <div class="hero-text">
<p class="hero-title">Monitoring coastal landscapes</p>
<p class="hero-subtitle">Ecology, remote sensing and geospatial analysis</p>
  </div>
</div>

<p class="intro-line">Welcome to my personal webpage, a space for sharing projects, fieldwork, visualizations and ideas.</p>

---

<p class="updates-section-title">Recent Activity</p>

::: {.update-card}

## Quantifying *Rugulopteryx* beach wrack accumulation

::: {.update-card__meta}
30 April 2026
:::

*Rugulopteryx okamurae* was detected at Arbeyal Beach, where it had been previously observed. Here, we attempt to quantify the volume of the wrack accumulation.

[Open the project](https://fer-at-sea.github.io/rugu_arbeyal/){.update-card__link target="_blank" rel="noopener"}
:::

::: {.update-card}

## EOPF Zarr Community Notebook Competition

::: {.update-card__meta}
29 April 2026
:::

The notebook *Cloud-native intertidal vegetation monitoring with Sentinel-2 and EOPF Zarr*, by Fernando García-González and Pablo González González, was selected as one of the winning entries in the EOPF Zarr Community Notebook Competition, part of the EOPF Toolkit initiative developed with ESA, Development Seed and Sparkgeo. All selected notebooks will be integrated into the [EOPF 101 online book](https://eopf-toolkit.github.io/eopf-101/). Until the integration is completed the notebook can be accessed on [GitHub](https://github.com/fer-at-sea/community-notebook-competition/blob/fer-at-sea-submission/submissions/cloud_native_intertidal_vegetation_monitoring_with_sentinel2_and_EOPF_Zarr/submission.ipynb) 


[Open the post on LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/feed/update/urn:li:share:7455232416664707072/){.update-card__link target="_blank" rel="noopener"}
:::


::: {.update-card}

## Coastal AI initiative

::: {.update-card__meta}
14 February 2024
:::

 Kick-off of the collaboration between the Artificial Intelligence Center (AIC) of the University of Oviedo and the Seagrass Ecology Group of the Spanish Institute of Oceanography (IEO-CSIC). Explore the ongoing work of Coastal AI and our GitHub organization.


[Coastal AI on GitHub](https://github.com/coastal-AI){.update-card__link target="_blank" rel="noopener"}
:::
---
